The error occurs at the very end of the proof, where there's a `sorry` tactic.
The state before the `sorry` tactic is:
```
╬▒ : Type u_1
╬▓ : Type u_2
G : Graph ╬▒ ╬▓
M : Set ╬▓
P : WList ╬▒ ╬▓
hM : @Graph.IsMatching ╬▒ ╬▓ G M
h : @Graph.IsAugPath ╬▒ ╬▓ M G hM P
ŌŖó V(G, M) ŌŖå V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet)
```

The `sorry` tactic is used when the proof is incomplete. The goal is to prove `V(G, M) ŌŖå V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet)`.
Looking at the available hypotheses:
- `hM : @Graph.IsMatching ╬▒ ╬▓ G M`: This states that `M` is a matching in `G`.
- `h : @Graph.IsAugPath ╬▒ ╬▓ M G hM P`: This states that `P` is an augmenting path with respect to `M` in `G`.

The definition of `IsAugPath` implies that the endpoints of `P` are not in `V(G, M)`, and `P` alternates between edges in `M` and edges not in `M`. The set `symmDiff M P.edgeSet` is the symmetric difference of the sets `M` and `P.edgeSet` (the set of edges in the path `P`). This is the set of edges that are in `M` or in `P.edgeSet` but not in both. The goal is to show that the vertices covered by `M` are a subset of the vertices covered by this symmetric difference, since any vertex covered by `M` is either an endpoint of an edge in `M` (and thus in the symmetric difference) or is in `M` but not in `P` (which would be an odd component, hence in the symmetric difference).

We can use the lemma `IsAugmentingPath.mem_symmDiff` which directly states this subset relation.

P : WList ╬▒ ╬▓
hM : @Graph.IsMatching ╬▒ ╬▓ G M

ŌŖó V(G, M) ŌŖå V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet)

The `sorry` tactic is used when the proof is incomplete. The goal is to prove `V(G, M) ŌŖå V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet)`.
Looking at the available hypotheses:
- `hM : @Graph.IsMatching ╬▒ ╬▓ G M`: This states that `M` is a matching in `G`.
- `h : @Graph.IsAugPath ╬▒ ╬▓ M G hM P`: This states that `P` is an augmenting path with respect to `M` in `G`.

The definition of `IsAugPath` implies that the endpoints of `P` are not in `V(G, M)`, and `P` alternates between edges in `M` and edges not in `M`. The set `symmDiff M P.edgeSet` is the symmetric difference of the sets `M` and `P.edgeSet` (the set of edges in the path `P`). This is the set of edges that are in `M` or in `P.edgeSet` but not in both. The goal is to show that the vertices covered by `M` are a subset of the vertices covered by this symmetric difference, since any vertex covered by `M` is either an endpoint of an edge in `M` (and thus in the symmetric difference) or is in `M` but not in `P` (which would be an odd component, hence in the symmetric difference).

We can use the lemma `IsAugmentingPath.mem_symmDiff` which directly states this subset relation.
